text.title: SSH Hardening Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the SSH Hardening methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Before reloading sshd with a new config: (a) keep the editing session OPEN, (b) reload sshd, (c) open a SECOND terminal and SSH in fresh, (d) only when the second login succeeds, close the first session. NEVER reload + logout in one move.
rule.rationale: A typo (or `Match User` block that excludes the operator) survives the reload; the still-open original session is the only escape hatch.

rule.statement: PasswordAuthentication=no, ChallengeResponseAuthentication=no, KbdInteractiveAuthentication=no. Only PubkeyAuthentication=yes. AuthenticationMethods=publickey.
rule.rationale: Password brute-force is the dominant attack vector; key-only eliminates the class.

rule.statement: AllowUsers (or AllowGroups) explicitly names every account permitted to SSH. PermitRootLogin=no. Default-deny posture.
rule.rationale: Adding a non-SSH service user (e.g. postgres) shouldn't accidentally expose an SSH login.

rule.statement: KexAlgorithms, Ciphers, MACs, HostKeyAlgorithms restricted to the Mozilla 'Modern' set (curve25519-sha256, chacha20-poly1305, aes*-gcm, ed25519). No legacy algorithms.
rule.rationale: Old algorithms (CBC ciphers, ssh-rsa with SHA-1) have known weaknesses and are deprecated.

rule.statement: Hardening MUST live in `/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/99-hardening.conf` (override drop-in) — NEVER directly edit `/etc/ssh/sshd_config`. Distro upgrades preserve drop-ins.
rule.rationale: Direct edits to /etc/ssh/sshd_config are squashed on package upgrade; drop-ins survive.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-ssh-hardening.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
